questions.text: The National Emergency Number Association (NENA) is conducting this survey to collect data from Public Safety Answering Points (PSAP) in the United States and Canada to evaluate and update call processing and staffing guidelines in response to evolving operational demands, emerging technologies, and increasing service complexity. The results will identify national trends, challenges, and effective practices to support data-driven decisions and the development of practical modern staffing recommendations that enhance efficiency, sustainability, and service quality. The survey takes approximately 20 to 30 minutes to complete. Participants are encouraged to have relevant staffing, call volume, and organization available, and may use the 'Save' feature to return later if needed. The deadline for completion is 07/31/2026. This initial phase focuses on core operations such as call processing, dispatching, and frontline FTE staffing. Future phases will include support and administrative functions. Broad participation is essential to ensure representative results. All responses will be analyzed in aggregate to support research and guideline development.
Please select your state or province from the list below:
questions.description: While the survey is anonymous, to assist in gathering data that can be used at a State or Provincial level, we would like to capture the state your PSAP is located in.

questions.text: What is the base population serviced by the PSAP?
questions.description: The base population allows us to see the population that is regularly serviced by the PSAP.
questions.data_location: This data can be gathered from your governing organization's census data

questions.text: What is the peak population serviced by the PSAP?
questions.description: The peak population allows us to see the influence of visitors and transient populations on PSAP operations.
questions.data_location: This data can be gathered from your governing organization's census data

questions.text: What are most common factors contiubting to the population variation? (Select all that apply)
questions.description: Understanding the factors that contribute to population variation can help identify operational challenges and staffing needs.
questions.data_location: This data can be gathered from your governing organization's demographic knowledge.

questions.text: Select the PSAP classification:
questions.description: We would like this data to determine the differences in operations and staffing between PSAP classifications.

questions.text: What services does the PSAP provide (select one or more):
questions.description: This allows the working groups to understand the variety of services provided by PSAPs and how that may influence operaations and staffing.

questions.text: Please list any additional services that you provide:
questions.description: We would like to know what services beyond calltaking and dispatching that you provide to understand the variety of services provided by PSAPs and how that may influence operations and staffing.

questions.text: What is the PSAP operational governance:
questions.description: This allows us to understand how to compare the same types of PSAPs and how their staffing needs may change due to other factors.
options: Federal Government (Airport, Tribal, LEO, Miliary, Other)

questions.text: Which specific agencies fall under this governance:

anyOf: Federal Government (Airport, Tribal, LEO, Miliary, Other)

questions.text: What agencies does the PSAP answer calls and dispatch for and how many of each?
questions.description: We would like to know how many agencies a PSAP handles during the course of their business

questions.text: Types of calls answered by PSAP:
questions.description: Knowing the type or traffic that the PSAP handles will help with creating models that address the work that the center does

questions.text: Total of 9-1-1 calls answered in the last calendar year:
questions.data_location: This information should come from your phone software.

questions.text: Total of all 10-digit emergency calls answered in the last calendar year:
questions.data_location: If you have 10-digit emergency lines, the call volume will be found in your phone software. If you do not, them leave this blank

questions.text: Total of all non-emergency calls answered in the last calendar year:
questions.data_location: This information should come from your phone software.

questions.text: Total of all SMS/Text sessions answered in the last calendar year:
questions.data_location: This information should come from your phone software.

questions.text: Total of all TTY/TDD sessions answered in the last calendar year:
questions.data_location: This information should come from your phone software.

questions.text: Total number of Law Enforcement events processed in the last calendar year:
questions.data_location: This data should come from your Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD) software.

questions.text: Total number of Fire events processed in the last calendar year:
questions.data_location: This data should come from your Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD) software.

questions.text: Total number of EMS events processed in the last calendar year:
questions.data_location: This data should come from your Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD) software.

questions.text: Total number of other events processed in the last calendar year:
questions.data_location: This data may come from your Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD) software or other relevant sources.

questions.text: Estimated radio keyups in the last calendar year:
questions.data_location: This information should come from your radio communication logs or other relevant sources.

questions.text: Average number of radio keyups per hour in the last calendar year:
questions.data_location: This information should come from your radio communication logs or other relevant sources.

questions.text: How many calls were transferred in the last calendar year?
questions.data_location: This data should come from your Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD) software.

questions.text: Of the calls transferred, how many were transferred to a secondary PSAP?
questions.data_location: This data may be sourced from your Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD) software and/or your phone software.

questions.text: Number of Funded Operational FTEs (Full Time Equivalents)
questions.description: We would like to know the number of telecommunicator and supervisor positions you currently have in your budget.

questions.text: Number of Telecommunicator FTEs in use
questions.description: We also would like to know how many of those budgeted positions are currently hired and working.

questions.text: Number of Supervisor FTEs in use
questions.description: We also would like to know how many of those budgeted supervisor positions are currently hired and working.

questions.text: What is your optimum number of Telecommunicator FTEs (if fully staffed)?
questions.description: This allows us to gauge the distribution of expected staffing levels when compared to the call volume and other operational factors.

questions.text: What is your optimum number of Supervisor FTEs (if fully staffed)?
questions.description: This allows us to gauge the distribution of expected staffing levels when compared to the call volume and other operational factors.

questions.text: What is the length of shifts in hours?
questions.description: This allows us to understand the distribution of shift lengths and how that may influence staffing needs and scheduling practices.

questions.text: Minimum number of telecommunicator FTEs per shift
questions.descriptions: This allows us to understand what your centre considers to be viable minimal staffing levels for telecommunicators and how that may influence scheduling practices and service quality.

questions.text: Minimum number of supervisor FTEs per shift
questions.description: This allows us to understand what your centre considers to be viable minimal staffing levels for supervisors and how that may influence scheduling practices and service quality.

questions.text: Maximum number of telecommunicator FTEs per shift
questions.description: This allows us to understand what your centre considers to be maximum staffing levels for telecommunicators and how that may influence scheduling practices and service quality.

questions.text: Maximum number of supervisor FTEs per shift
questions.description: This allows us to understand what your centre considers to be maximum staffing levels for supervisors and how that may influence scheduling practices and service quality.

questions.text: What is your average phone answer time for 9-1-1 calls in seconds? (From presentation to answer)
questions.description: This will allow us to understand how staffing levels may influence phone answer times and how that may influence service quality.
questions.data_location: This information should come from your phone software.

questions.text: What is your average phone answer time for non-emergency calls in seconds? (From presentation to answer)
questions.description: This will allow us to understand how staffing levels may influence phone answer times and how that may influence service quality.
questions.data_location: This information should come from your phone software.

questions.text: What is your average call processing time for 9-1-1 calls in seconds? (From answer to dispatch)
questions.description: This will allow us to understand how staffing levels may influence call processing times and how that may influence service quality.
questions.data_location: This information should come from your Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD) software.

questions.text: What is your average call processing time for non-emergency calls in seconds? (From answer to dispatch)
questions.description: This will allow us to understand how staffing levels may influence call processing times and how that may influence service quality.
questions.data_location: This information should come from your Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD) software.

questions.text: What is the average number of calls handled per telecommunicator FTE per shift?
questions.description: This will allow us to understand how staffing levels may influence the number of calls handled per telecommunicator and how that may influence service quality.
questions.data_location: This information should come from your phone software or your Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD) software.

questions.text: Thank you for completing the NENA 2024 PSAP Survey. Your participation is greatly appreciated and will help NENA better understand staffing and call processing standards. Your feedback is valuable throughout the industry and support meaningful solutions, advocacy, and future planning for the 9-1-1 community.
